ideas.rationale: Aligns with Memoria preferring one shared local base model with cheap task adapters over many fine-tunes; if Memoria ever needs task specialization (e.g., claim classification), prefix/LoRA-style adapters fit the single-GPU budget better than full fine-tuning.

ideas.idea: Zero-shot transfer to unseen attribute labels via GloVe cosine-similarity mapping to the nearest seen label's prefix
ideas.rationale: A cheap, deterministic way to handle a novel category/tag without retraining; analogous to Memoria mapping a new keyphrase/tag candidate to the closest existing controlled-vocabulary tag via embedding similarity at the pre-file gate.

ideas.idea: Length-ratio and other control tokens to calibrate output length/style at inference
ideas.rationale: Useful for Memoria's disposable projection engine and structured-capture summaries where the PI may want a controllable target length; implement as inference-time control tokens, no training needed.

ideas.idea: Use of external human-eval leaderboard (GENIE) instead of author-run evaluation for faithfulness
ideas.rationale: Reinforces Memoria's 'PI approves' governance: prefer independent/standardized evaluation of generated notes over engine self-report; ROUGE-style metrics shown unreliable for single-reference faithfulness, supporting Memoria's faithfulness-over-flash stance.

file: Cobbe et al. - 2021 - Training verifiers to solve math word problems.md
title: Training Verifiers to Solve Math Word Problems (GSM8K)
one_line: Introduces GSM8K and shows that training a separate verifier to score sampled solutions and selecting the highest-ranked one yields the gain of a ~30x larger model, with token-level verifiers and a generate-then-judge pipeline.

ideas.idea: Generator-verifier separation: a distinct verifier judges candidate outputs rather than trusting the generator
ideas.rationale: This is the formal ML grounding of Memoria's core 'engines write, agents judge, PI approves' gate; separating the producing engine from an independent judging engine (and keeping them distinct to avoid the generator overfitting to its own outputs) is exactly the structural-review-gate architecture.

ideas.idea: Sample-and-rank: generate N candidates, score each, return the top-ranked (with majority vote among top-k for robustness)
ideas.rationale: Directly buildable in Memoria's discovery/triage and projection paths: generate multiple candidate notes/extractions, score with a relevance/faithfulness verifier, surface the best — a cheap test-time-compute lever that beats scaling the base model, ideal under the local-model constraint.

ideas.idea: Token-level (per-step) verification scoring each reasoning step, not just the final answer
ideas.rationale: Maps onto Memoria's atomic-claim and claim-sentence classification: judging each extracted claim/step independently (rather than the whole note) gives finer-grained, more robust judgments and is inherently interpretable (visualize where confidence drops) — useful for the contradictions/uncertainty-flag pipeline.

ideas.idea: Token-level verifiers are interpretable: per-token confidence visualizes exactly where the verifier loses trust
ideas.rationale: Memoria's read-only inspector / gate dashboard can render per-claim or per-sentence verifier confidence so the PI sees WHY a note was flagged, supporting human approval and the diagnostics plane.

ideas.idea: False positives: solutions reach the right answer via flawed reasoning, fooling answer-only verifiers
ideas.rationale: Strong argument for Memoria to verify the reasoning/warrant in the argument graph, not just the conclusion; an extracted claim can be 'correct' yet unsupported — the gate must check derivation/warrant, echoing the contradiction and supersession machinery.

ideas.idea: A small verifier can effectively judge a large generator's outputs (verifier need not be as large)
ideas.rationale: Cost-decisive for Memoria: a cheap local judging model (small qwen) can gate outputs from a larger/more expensive generation step, fitting the single-GPU budget and the test-env model split.

ideas.idea: Diminishing/negative returns past ~400 samples — adversarial solutions begin to fool the verifier
ideas.rationale: Memoria should cap candidate generation in its sample-and-rank loops; more samples eventually surface verifier-gaming artifacts, so a modest N plus PI approval is safer than unbounded search.

ideas.idea: Auxiliary LM objective alongside verification improves the verifier; dropout strongly regularizes
ideas.rationale: These are training-time tricks for fine-tuning a verifier model; Memoria favors prompted local models over training its own verifier, so the specific training recipe is out of scope.

file: Coopamootoo and Groß - 2017 - Why privacy is all but forgotten an empirical study of privacy & sharing attitude.md
title: Why Privacy Is All But Forgotten: An Empirical Study of Privacy & Sharing Attitude
one_line: An empirical content-analysis study (N=60 MTurk free-form responses) showing privacy vs. sharing attitudes are distinct affective constructs — fear predicts privacy framing, happiness predicts sharing — with a tone-based logistic classifier discriminating them at ~84-93%.

ideas.idea: A priori codebook with line-by-line open coding, two independent coders, and inter-rater reliability (Cohen's kappa) for qualitative-to-quantitative coding
ideas.rationale: Memoria's claim/aspect extraction is effectively automated coding; borrowing the discipline of a defined codebook (controlled label set), dual judgment, and an agreement metric supports the gate — e.g., two engine passes plus an agreement check before a note is written, with kappa-style reliability on the classifier.

ideas.idea: Discriminative logistic regression over emotional-tone features for binary text classification
ideas.rationale: A small bespoke MTurk-tuned classifier for privacy-vs-sharing affect; off-domain for a research-paper vault and superseded by LLM-based classification Memoria already uses.

ideas.idea: Direct open-ended questioning (affirmative, non-priming prompts) elicits richer, less biased responses than concern-leading questionnaires
ideas.rationale: Applies to Memoria's structured-capture forms: phrase PI capture prompts affirmatively and neutrally to avoid priming the response, yielding richer notes — a small UX/design insight, not a system feature.

ideas.idea: Attitude-behavior gap / privacy paradox as a measurement artifact (paradox appears mainly when attitude is compared to a mismatched behavior)
ideas.rationale: Social-psychology theory about human self-disclosure; no buildable component for a single-researcher knowledge system and not in Memoria's domain.

ideas.idea: Cross-validation of the classifier on a second held-out sample (N'=54) before claiming generalization
ideas.rationale: Reinforces Memoria's 'verify the inference, not just the file' principle: any auto-classifier (triage ranker, relevance scorer) should be validated on held-out data before trusting it in the gate.

file: Cornelio et al. - 2025 - The need for verification in AI-driven scientific discovery.md
title: The Need for Verification in AI-Driven Scientific Discovery
one_line: A review arguing that AI (LLMs, symbolic regression, neuro-symbolic models) can flood science with hypotheses but that rigorous, transparent verification — logic derivability, constraint/empirical checking against background theory — must be the cornerstone, surveying generator-verifier and theory-guided frameworks.

ideas.idea: Verification is the bottleneck, not generation: abundant AI hypotheses overwhelm validation pipelines, so widening verification is the priority
ideas.rationale: This is the thesis behind Memoria's entire governance stance — the nightly proactive discovery loop generates abundantly, and the structural-review gate IS the verification bottleneck deliberately widened by automation; the paper validates prioritizing the gate over raw generation throughput.

ideas.idea: Generator-verifier paradigm (AI-Descartes): any hypothesis generator paired with a formal verifier that checks consistency against background knowledge
ideas.rationale: Maps onto Memoria's argument graph with warrants and contradictions dashboard: extracted claims (hypotheses) should be checked against the vault's existing accepted claims/background (the PI's prior notes) before being written — a modular generator-then-verifier gate.

ideas.idea: 'Reasoning distance' — quantify the discrepancy between a candidate claim's predictions and what is derivable from accepted axioms/theory, and rank by it
ideas.rationale: Memoria can compute an analogous consistency score: how far a new claim is from being entailed/supported by existing accepted claims, feeding contradiction detection, supersession, and the discovery relevance/triage ranker.

ideas.idea: Integrate theory at generation time (AI-Hilbert) vs. post-hoc verification — embedding constraints during generation prunes the search space
ideas.rationale: Suggests Memoria's pre-file similarity gate and retriever/scout could inject the PI's existing accepted-claim context into extraction prompts so candidates are born consistent, not just filtered after — but Memoria keeps a separate judging step, so this is partial borrow, not full adoption.

ideas.idea: RLVR verifies only the final answer, not intermediate steps; models reach right answers via wrong reasoning, and rewards are idiosyncratic/unguaranteed
ideas.rationale: Reinforces verifying warrants/derivations in Memoria's argument graph, not just claim conclusions; cautions against trusting any single LLM 'self-verified' output and supports an independent, transparent judging engine over model self-report.

ideas.idea: LLMs hallucinate citations and fabricate references (legal cases, biomedical refs)
ideas.rationale: Directly motivates Memoria's Zotero/citekey backbone and deterministic ingest: every claim must be anchored to a real, resolvable source; the gate should reject claims whose citations don't resolve in the vault.

ideas.idea: Verification strategies are domain-heterogeneous (formal in physics, ontological/experimental in biology, probabilistic in clinical) but all rest on structured iterative reasoning
ideas.rationale: Memoria spans health/science domains (per ADRs); its verifier should support multiple evidence types — logical entailment, empirical support, and probabilistic/statistical claims with uncertainty flags — rather than one rigid check, matching Memoria's uncertainty-flag design.

ideas.idea: Preserve organic 'mistakes'/diversity — over-homogenization of method by AI is a risk
ideas.rationale: A design caution for Memoria: the gate should surface and let the PI keep speculative/contradictory claims (flagged), not auto-prune everything uncertain, preserving serendipity in the discovery loop.

ideas.idea: Benchmarks must test true open-ended discovery, not memorized rediscovery (memorization vs reasoning concern)
ideas.rationale: Informs how Memoria evaluates its own extraction/triage quality: build held-out, vault-specific evaluation rather than relying on generic benchmarks the local model may have memorized — supports Memoria's L5 quality-testing split.

ideas.idea: Formal proof assistants (Lean/Coq) give guarantees but can't be broadly applied because the natural sciences lack an agreed axiom set
ideas.rationale: Full formal theorem-proving is out of scope and infeasible for a heterogeneous personal research vault on a local model; Memoria's gate is LLM-judged plus deterministic checks, not a proof assistant.

file: Martinengo et al. - 2022 - Conversational agents in health care scoping review of their behavior change techniques and underpi.md
title: Conversational Agents in Health Care: Scoping Review of Their Behavior Change Techniques and Underpinning Theory
one_line: A PRISMA-ScR scoping review of 47 studies coding behavior-change techniques (BCTTv1 taxonomy) in health-care conversational agents, using frequent-itemset mining to find which BCTs co-occur.

ideas.idea: Code each intervention against a fixed 93-item taxonomy (BCTTv1) grouped into 16 categories, with two independent reviewers and consensus arbitration for disagreements.
ideas.rationale: The pattern of mapping free-text descriptions onto a controlled vocabulary with independent dual-coding plus an arbiter mirrors Memoria's claim-sentence classification and MASSW aspect tagging. The dual-reviewer/arbiter structure is a model for the 'engines write, agents judge, PI approves' gate: an engine proposes a taxonomy code, an agent cross-checks, the PI is final arbiter. The specific health BCT taxonomy itself is off-domain and not adopted.

ideas.idea: Frequent-itemset mining (Apriori, support/confidence thresholds) to discover which techniques recurrently co-occur across a corpus.
ideas.rationale: Memoria could run FIM/association-rule mining over the per-paper aspect/claim-type tags it already extracts to surface 'these techniques/aspects tend to appear together' patterns for the discovery loop and dashboards. It is a cheap, deterministic, single-machine analysis fitting Memoria's constraints. Borrow the mechanism, not the health content.

ideas.idea: Observation that studies using the same theory still employ wildly different technique sets, i.e., the stated theory does not predict the actual components.
ideas.rationale: A domain finding about behavior-change theory with no transferable mechanism for Memoria's architecture.

ideas.idea: Categorizing agents by modality (rule-based vs AI, embodied vs text, coach-like persona) and correlating with clinical domain.
ideas.rationale: Concerns conversational-agent UI/persona design for patient-facing health apps; Memoria is a single-researcher MCP-only knowledge system with no patient-facing chatbot, so this is off-domain.

file: McGee and Martin - 2011 - “You would not believe what I have to go through to prove my intellectual value!” stereotype managem.md
title: “You Would Not Believe What I Have to Go Through to Prove My Intellectual Value!” Stereotype Management Among Academically Successful Black Mathematics and Engineering Students
one_line: A qualitative life-story interview study (23 students, CRT lens) introducing 'stereotype management' as a tactical, motivating response to racial stereotypes among high-achieving Black STEM students.

ideas.idea: Open-coding of life-story interviews into belief->action pairs, then iterative recategorization into higher-level themes via member checks.
ideas.rationale: This is standard qualitative thematic coding on human-subjects interview data, an entirely different modality and pipeline from Memoria's deterministic ingest of academic-paper PDFs into atomic claims; nothing in the method transfers.

ideas.idea: Substantive theory: stereotype threat vs stereotype management; race/identity in STEM education.
ideas.rationale: Off-domain sociology-of-education content with no architectural or technical insight for a personal research-knowledge system.

file: Meng et al. - 2026 - ScientistOne towards human-level autonomous research via chain-of-evidence.md
title: ScientistOne: Towards Human-Level Autonomous Research via Chain-of-Evidence
one_line: Introduces Chain-of-Evidence (every research claim must trace through a recorded evidence chain to a grounding source), an autonomous research system that satisfies it by construction, and a four-check post-hoc CoE Integrity Audit.

ideas.idea: Chain-of-Evidence principle: every claim must trace, through a recorded chain, to a grounding source — framed as an ACID-style standard for what 'verifiable' means, independent of how the artifact is built.
ideas.rationale: This is the formalization of Memoria's faithfulness-over-flash pillar. Memoria already extracts atomic claims and tracks typed relations/warrants; adopting CoE means every claim note must carry an explicit provenance edge to its source span (citekey + locator), and the structural review gate should refuse to write a claim that lacks one. Directly buildable as a required field/edge in the claim schema.

ideas.idea: Claim taxonomy with per-type evidence-chain shapes: citation claims (must resolve to a real scholarly record consistent with description), numerical claims (must trace to a recorded output/log line), methodological claims (must resolve to implementation), conclusion claims (must derive from supporting claims via verifiable reasoning).
ideas.rationale: Refines Memoria's claim-sentence classification with a verification-shape per type. Memoria can type each extracted claim and route it to the matching check: citation->record-linkage against Zotero/Semantic Scholar; numerical->trace to source table/figure span; conclusion->require links to its supporting claims in the argument graph. This sharpens the uncertainty-flag and argument-graph machinery.

ideas.idea: Ground–Critic–Resolve loop: deterministic tag validation (Ground) + LLM audit of what determinism can't catch, e.g. overclaims/contradictions (Critic) + rewrite dropping unsupported claims (Resolve), iterating to convergence; only a draft with zero blocking violations is promoted.
ideas.rationale: This is essentially Memoria's 'engines write, agents judge, PI approves' gate made concrete for claim-writing. The deterministic-first, LLM-judge-second split matches Memoria's deterministic-ingest + agent-judgment design and the MCP-only sandbox (the Critic is a judge with no write access; only a clean draft passes the gate). Adopt as the gating loop before a claim note is written.

ideas.idea: Reference Verification via multi-API resolution (Semantic Scholar, arXiv, OpenAlex, CrossRef) with LLM disambiguation of near-misses; zero-hallucination achieved by making every citation originate from a cached API call in the evidence chain rather than model memory.
ideas.rationale: Memoria has a citekey/Zotero backbone and a record-linkage entity-dedup component; the 'every reference must originate from a resolved API record cached in the evidence chain' rule is a directly buildable hard invariant for the pre-file similarity/structural gate, preventing fabricated citations. The multi-source resolver + LLM near-miss disambiguation is the concrete mechanism.

ideas.idea: Numerical Claim Provenance Rate (CPR): each sentence with a number gets a {source: log:line} tag, and a verifier extracts and matches the number against the referenced source within a tolerance.
ideas.rationale: Memoria ingests papers (not running its own experiments), so the source is a table/figure/sentence span rather than an execution log, but the inline-source-tag + tolerance-match verifier is a concrete, cheap check the structural gate can run on every numerical claim it extracts. Borrow the tagging+match mechanism, adapt the source type.

ideas.idea: Forensic post-hoc audit (Score Verification, Specification Violation, Reference Verification, Method–Code Alignment) applied uniformly across systems via an adapter that normalizes artifacts into a common bundle, with majority-vote LLM judgment to reduce noise.
ideas.rationale: Memoria can run a periodic forensic audit over already-written claim notes (not just at write time): a static-HTML admin report scoring what fraction of claims still resolve to live source records, flagging drift. Majority-vote LLM judging fits as an offline batch on cheap local models. The score/spec/method-code checks themselves assume the system runs experiments, so only the audit pattern is borrowed.

ideas.idea: Empirical finding that paper quality is bottlenecked by soundness, not writing clarity — 'papers read well but do not withstand methodological scrutiny,' and verifiable papers are judged better.
ideas.rationale: Justifies Memoria prioritizing faithfulness/provenance over fluent prose surfaces. Reinforces the contradictions dashboard and argument-graph emphasis: surface a claim's soundness state (supported/contested/superseded) prominently rather than polished summaries. An insight, not a buildable mechanism.

file: Merrill et al. - 2026 - Transforming wearable data into personal health insights using large language model agents.md
title: Transforming Wearable Data into Personal Health Insights Using Large Language Model Agents (PHIA)
one_line: PHIA, a ReAct-based LLM agent that answers open-ended personal-health questions over wearable time-series by generating Python (in a sandbox) and web search, beating a single-shot code-gen baseline in a 650-hour human eval.

ideas.idea: Push numerical reasoning out of the LLM into generated code run in a sandbox: text-only numerical reasoning is unreliable, so the agent emits Python whose outputs are factual and arithmetic-precise, and the LLM only sees aggregated results.
ideas.rationale: Strong evidence for Memoria's deterministic-engines pillar: any numerical/statistical analysis over the vault (claim counts, co-occurrence, similarity scores) should be done by deterministic engine code, not asked of the LLM. Tension: PHIA gives the agent code execution, which Memoria's MCP-only sandbox forbids — so Memoria realizes this as engines (not agents) running the code, with agents only consuming results via MCP. Borrow the principle, not the agent-runs-code architecture.

ideas.idea: ReAct Thought->Act->Observe loop with iterative self-correction: PHIA recovers from fatal code errors 11.4% of the time and halves the error rate vs single-shot code-gen using the same base model.
ideas.rationale: Memoria's Hermes agents already operate in tool-use loops; the quantified finding that iterative observe-and-retry markedly cuts error/hallucination rates supports an explicit retry-on-failure loop in ingest/discovery engines. The recovery happens in engine code (re-run on tool error), keeping agents MCP-only.

ideas.idea: Few-shot exemplar selection by embedding all queries (sentence-T5), K-means to ~20 clusters, and taking the query nearest each centroid as a representative demonstration.
ideas.rationale: A cheap, local, deterministic recipe for picking diverse few-shot exemplars that Memoria can reuse when prompting local models for claim extraction/classification or triage — cluster the candidate space and sample centroids rather than hand-curating. Fits the cheap/open-model constraint.

ideas.idea: Web-search tool integration to ground answers in up-to-date authoritative sources, with raters valuing responses that connect personal data to external domain norms.
ideas.rationale: Parallels Memoria's discovery/retriever profile: insights are stronger when a vault claim is contextualized against external literature. Memoria already has discovery; the lesson is to surface external-grounding links alongside an internal finding. The 'web search' channel for Memoria is its scholarly-API discovery, not general web.

ideas.idea: Code execution reduces raw-data exposure to the LLM (model only sees aggregates), a privacy-by-aggregation benefit.
ideas.rationale: Reinforces Memoria's local-first/least-exposure stance: engines compute over the full vault and hand agents only the minimal projected/aggregated view they need. Consistent with the disposable projection engine and MCP-only sandbox.

ideas.idea: Synthetic-data generation (CPAR + Gaussian Copula) to evaluate over private wearable streams; template-based objective-query generation for automatic eval.
ideas.rationale: Wearable-time-series synthesis is off-domain for a paper-ingest system. The template-driven query/answer generation for benchmarking is mildly interesting but Memoria's eval targets are claim-extraction faithfulness, not numerical query answering, so not adopted.

ideas.idea: Harm-avoidance / defer-to-professional guardrails for health advice (>99% rated harmless).
ideas.rationale: Patient-safety guardrails for a health-advice chatbot; Memoria is single-researcher and not giving health advice, so out of scope.

file: Min et al. - 2022 - Noisy channel language model prompting for few-shot text classification.md
title: Noisy Channel Language Model Prompting for Few-Shot Text Classification
one_line: Proposes channel models (score P(input|label)·P(label) via Bayes instead of P(label|input)) for few-shot LM prompting, showing they beat direct models mainly via lower variance, better worst-case accuracy, robustness to label imbalance, and ability to predict unseen labels.

ideas.idea: Channel (generative) prompting: reparameterize classification as scoring how well each label 'explains' the input (P(x|c)), which forces the model to account for every input word and amplifies signal in the low-data regime.
ideas.rationale: Memoria does many few-shot, frozen-local-model classification tasks (claim-sentence classification, MASSW aspect tagging, keyphrase candidacy, triage labels) where labeled data is tiny and the model is qwen2.5:7b with no fine-tuning. The channel formulation is a no-training-cost scoring change that improves stability exactly in this regime — directly applicable to Memoria's classification engines. Borrow as an alternative scoring head, validate on the test rig (RTX 4060 Ti, local models).

ideas.idea: Channel models are markedly more robust to imbalanced training labels and high |C|, and can predict labels unseen during training (no label-space overfit).
ideas.rationale: Memoria's tag/aspect/relation-type vocabularies are large and skewed (some claim types/relation types are rare), and new types get added over time — precisely the imbalanced/large-|C|/unseen-label conditions where the paper recommends channel prompting. This is a concrete decision rule for when Memoria's classifiers should use channel scoring.

ideas.idea: Ensemble-based demonstrations: score each of K demos independently and multiply, instead of concatenating — O(K) memory, removes order sensitivity, beats concat for direct models by ~7%.
ideas.rationale: Memoria runs prompts on a 16GB GPU where context length and prompt-order sensitivity matter; the ensemble-of-single-demos trick reduces memory and removes the order-dependence that plagues few-shot prompting. A cheap, buildable improvement for any few-shot classification engine.

ideas.idea: Stability framing: report worst-case accuracy and variance over verbalizers/seeds, not just average; worst-case matters for high-risk use.
ideas.rationale: Memoria's faithfulness pillar means a classifier that is sometimes wildly wrong is worse than a steadily mediocre one. Adopt worst-case/variance reporting (over prompt/verbalizer/seed variants) as a standard metric in Memoria's classification-engine evaluation, feeding the structural-gate confidence and uncertainty flags.

ideas.idea: Empirical guidance: head tuning (tuning only the LM head) is a strong, often-omitted baseline; prompt tuning matches full finetuning only at full data; method choice depends on K, |C|, balance, unseen-label needs.
ideas.rationale: A practical selection table for parameter-light adaptation. Memoria prefers frozen local models, but if it ever does light task-specific adaptation, this guides choosing prompt-tuning vs head-tuning by data regime. Useful as decision guidance, not a built feature.

ideas.idea: Specifics tied to GPT-2 / causal-LM prompting and the difficulty of channel models for masked LMs and non-classification tasks.
ideas.rationale: The masked-LM limitation and GPT-2-specific results are dated implementation details; Memoria uses modern instruction-tuned causal models. The conceptual channel approach transfers, but these particular constraints do not constrain Memoria.

file: Perez et al. - 2022 - Discovering language model behaviors with model-written evaluations.md
title: Discovering Language Model Behaviors with Model-Written Evaluations
one_line: LMs can cheaply generate large, diverse, high-quality evaluation datasets via a generate-then-discriminate (pg/pd) pipeline, with a separate model filtering for label-correctness.

ideas.idea: Generate-then-filter: one model generates label-conditioned items, a second discriminator keeps high-confidence ones
ideas.rationale: Directly the engines-write/agents-judge pattern; template for claim/relation/tag candidate generation + cheap judge before the gate.

ideas.idea: Discriminator confidence as a data-quality / uncertainty estimate
ideas.rationale: Maps to uncertainty flags + triage scoring; surfaces low-confidence items to the PI.

ideas.idea: Bootstrap eval sets by model-writing labeled examples, PI-validate a sample
ideas.rationale: Single-researcher can bootstrap eval fixtures without hand-writing hundreds.

ideas.idea: Embedding+UMAP+LM-labeled clusters as diversity/artifact diagnostic
ideas.rationale: Reuse BERTopic/clustering as a diagnostic for skew/dedup gaps.

ideas.idea: AI-safety behavioral eval content (power-seeking, corrigibility, personas)
ideas.rationale: Out of scope; MCP-only sandbox already neutralizes the agentic risks probed.
